messages.subject: [R-SIG-Finance] Fractals library
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I've been studying the paper Identification and Coding of Elliot Wave
Pattern, Vaghela, V, Gor, R.  It uses Williams Fractals Type I for trends
identification and associates it with the Awesome Indicator.  While it
isn't that complex I wonder if there would be a function (or library)
already available for computing its fractal. TIA.

Regards,

--
Andr? Luiz Tietbohl Ramos, PhD

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Finance] Multiline subtitle
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

Suppose I want to have a multiline subtitle using ggplot() with aligned
strings between first line and second line like a table.  I'm using the
approach below to no avail,

tickers.subtitle <- c("CPFE3.SA",  "CXSE3.SA", " ALUP11.SA",  "BBAS3.SA",  "
CMIG4.SA",  "VALE3.SA", " LEVE3.SA", "VIVT3.SA", "TAEE4.SA", "RANI3.SA", "
BRSR6.SA", "SAPR4.SA")
ticker.subtitle.weights <- c("20.0%",   "15.0%",  "15.0%",  "5.0%", "5.0%",
"5.7%", "5.7%", "5.7%", "5.7%", "5.7%", "5.7%", "5.7%")

port_cumulative_ret %>% ggplot(aes(x = date, y = cum_ret)) +
...
labs(
...
subtitle = paste('Ativos: ', tickers.subtitle,
                          '\nPesos: ', ticker.subtitle.weights)
)

Any suggestions?

TIA,

--
Andr? Luiz Tietbohl Ramos, PhD

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
